name: Best 6 Openmart Alternatives for Prospecting SMB Clients
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

If your agency targets SMBs and local businesses, you already know two things: (1) data quality matters more than fancy outreach sequences, and (2) most top-tier B2B databases are optimized for enterprise contacts, not local SMB signals. 

Most agencies don’t struggle with finding SMBs. They struggle with finding SMBs worth calling.

Sales reps waste 5–10 hours a week manually researching prospects, and nearly 3 out of 4 leads go nowhere. Not because SMBs don’t need help, but because the data is shallow, generic, or straight-up wrong.

Openmart is strong on local SMB signals, but it’s not the only option. This guide compares 6 practical Openmart alternatives for agency prospecting.

We’ve tested SMB-specific data quality and identified exactly which platforms give you the digital footprint intelligence that actually matters when selling to dentists, plumbers, restaurants, and retail shops.

TL;DR

  • Openmart is strong for local discovery, but agencies often switch due to credit-based pricing and limited sales workflows.
  • 6 Openmart alternatives: 
    • Synup OS
    • Apollo
    • Lusha
    • Leadfeeder
    • ZoomInfo
    • BuzzBoard
  • Synup OS and BuzzBoard offer the deepest SMB-specific data; Apollo, Lusha, and ZoomInfo skew enterprise; Leadfeeder is best for inbound intent.
  • For SMB agencies, the best tools combine accurate local data, digital gap insights, and end-to-end sales workflows, not just contacts.

Why Switch from Openmart?

Openmart is a focused AI prospecting/search product optimized for local businesses. While it’s good at what it claims, agencies often switch from Openmart for various reasons:

  • Credit anxiety: Like many prospecting platforms, Openmart operates on a credit-based model. Credits burn unevenly. Phone numbers cost more. And “verified” contacts still bounce even when they consume credits. 
  • Direct SMB signals & operational workflows in one place: Agencies usually need prospect lists, outreach templates, and pipelines in one place. Openmart excels at the top of the funnel, i.e., finding businesses and exporting contact lists. Once you find a lead, you’re exporting CSVs and duct-taping 3–4 tools just to move the deal forward.
  • Better SMB-specific enrichment: Agencies often need insights on local listings, open hours, review presence, and website gaps to create higher-converting outreach. Because knowing who to call isn’t enough. You need to know why they should care.

What Features to Look for in an Openmart Alternative?

When evaluating alternatives for SMB prospecting, prioritize these features:

  1. Local/SMB-specific signals: Listings, review presence, business category, opening hours, multi-location detection.
  2. Freshness & verification: How often the dataset is updated and how it’s verified.
  3. Search filters tuned to SMB needs: Industry, employee size, revenue band, and radius or zip filters.
  4. Outreach workflow: Ability to export or push prospects to sequences/CRMs.
  5. Affordability & credit pricing: Cost per prospect and scale economics for agencies.
  6. Integrations: Ability to sync with CRM, outreach, and agency OS.

SMB-friendly enrichment: Digital gap signals (e.g., lacks listings, no reviews) used to personalize outreach.
sections: synup-os11; apollo11; lusha11; leadfeeder11; zoominfo11; buzzboard11

seo.title: Best Openmart Alternatives for SMB Prospecting
seo.description: Relying solely on Openmart can restrict your lead pipeline. Explore six alternatives that support stronger SMB client prospecting.
